response.justification: The candidate is a strong senior web/full-stack engineer, but this role is a poor fit on nearly every hard requirement. The position demands native-level Japanese, which the resume does not list (English, Romanian, basic German), and that alone is disqualifying. Beyond language, the role centers on C for UNIX, Linux kernel investigation and modification, and embedded LAN-switch security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, L2/L3 networking) — the candidate's C/C++ exposure is brief and dated (a 7-month stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015), and there is no evidence of kernel work, network-security protocols, or switch/firmware development.
strengths: Long, varied engineering career with senior/staff-level roles and demonstrated ownership
Some systems-level experience: low-latency DLang services, Linux, and past C/C++ work at National Instruments
Strong engineering practices (TDD, CI/CD, testing tooling) and open-source contributions
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency, a stated hard requirement for the role
No embedded networking, Linux kernel, or security-protocol (MACsec, 802.1X) experience
Recent career is entirely web/full-stack (TypeScript, Node.js), far from C-based firmware development

key_factors.factor: Senior engineering experience and systems exposure
